title: cordia globosa
intro: Cordia globosa, also known as the 'Indian cherry' or 'Screwpine', is a tropical tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, globe-shaped fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cordia globosa include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and cholesterol levels. Cordia globosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, which are commonly used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
